cards.front: What is a tree in data structures?
cards.back: A tree is a non-linear data structure consisting of nodes with a value and zero or more child nodes, where each node has a unique key
A binary tree is a tree data structure in which each node has at most two children (i.e., left child and right child)

cards.front: What are the three main traversals in a binary tree?
cards.back: Inorder traversal (left-root-right), preorder traversal (root-left-right), and postorder traversal (left-right-root)

cards.front: What is a threaded binary tree?
cards.back: A threaded binary tree is a binary tree where each node has a reference (thread) to the left child, allowing for efficient traversal

cards.front: What is the purpose of traversing a tree?
cards.back: To perform operations like searching, inserting, deleting, and traversing the tree in a specific order
